subject: Re: Warp 1.0.7 Crashing on Particle Picking
body:
Can you please try replacing libtensorflow.dll in the installation 
directory with this 
one: https://www.dropbox.com/s/azh7598wvmb9prt/libtensorflow.zip?dl=0

On Thursday, March 12, 2020 at 5:24:04 PM UTC+1, Kendra wrote:
>
> Hi Dimitry,
>
> Thanks for the fast response.  As soon as you get a new version up, then 
> we will definitely try the install again to see if that is the cause. Stay 
> healthy (and not just because we want 1.0.7)! :)
>
> Cheers,
> Kendra
>
> On Thursday, March 12, 2020 at 5:03:40 PM UTC+1, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>
>> Hi Kendra,
>>
>> sorry this comes very very late, but I finally bumped into the same 
>> problem (I think) on one of the lab PCs a few days ago. The issue was with 
>> the CPU not supporting the AVX2 instruction set. Before 1.0.7, I used to 
>> compile TensorFlow without that flag, which is why it ran fine on the same 
>> hardware. I will revert to AVX for future versions. I will share an updated 
>> binary here as soon as I'm back in the lab on Monday. Hopefully the 
>> institute won't be shut down because of COVID19 by then.
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>>
>> On Thursday, March 12, 2020 at 4:34:32 PM UTC+1, Kendra wrote:
>>>
>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>
>>> As a continuation of this thread, we have tried again with a 1.0.7 
>>> install (using the installer from the warp-em website) on a different 
>>> computer today and Warp still crashes with particle picking selected.  We 
>>> updated the drivers, etc. today before the install.
>>>
>>> 2 x Intel Xeon E5-2667 v2, 3.30 GHz each
>>> 3 x GeForce GTX 1080 Ti with driver version 442.59 
>>> 128 GB RAM
>>> Windows 10 Pro N
>>>
>>> Errors in the Windows Event Viewer:
>>> Faulting application name: Warp.exe, version 1.0.8.0, time stamp: 
>>> 0x5d9cbc15
>>> Faulting module name: ucrtbase.dll, version 10.0.16299.15, time stamp: 
>>> 0x70f70cc4
>>> Exception code: 0xc0000409
>>> Fault offset: 0x000000000006b70e
>>> Faulting process ID: 0x26fc
>>> Faulting application start time: 0x01d5f87f50741d3b
>>> Faulting application path: C:\warp_107\Warp.exe
>>> Faulting module path: C:\Windows\System32\ucrtbase.dll
>>> Report ID: 3d0b5f41-8151-46f2-89f0-6b4b17d89023
>>> Faulting package full name:
>>> Faulting package-relative application ID:
>>>
>>> Do you have any insight as to why this might be happening? We are 
>>> interested in trying some of the newer functionalities of Warp, but can't 
>>> do that until we can install a working version beyond 1.0.5. 
>>>
>>> Thank you for any help/advice you (or anyone else in the community) can 
>>> give!
>>>
>>> Sincerely,
>>> Kendra Leigh
>>>
>>>
>>> On Friday, December 13, 2019 at 5:10:51 PM UTC+1, RRRuza wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Bumping this thread because we have the same problem: everything worked 
>>>> fine on 1.0.5, but on 1.0.6 and 1.0.7 Warp crashes when loading the model 
>>>> for particle picking.
>>>>
>>>> System specs (dedicated machine for Warp):
>>>> - Windows 10 Enterprise (64 bit)
>>>> - 2 x Intel Xeon E5-2670, 2.60 GHz each 
>>>> - 64 GB RAM
>>>> - 1 x GeForce GTX 1080 Ti (11 GB dedicated memory, NVIDIA driver 
>>>> version: 441.66)
>>>>
>>>>
